heading: Step 3: Create the index.
body:
**Input:**```shell
python create_index.py
```**Output:**```
New index name: default
```The MongoDB Search dateFacet type takes the following parameters:| Option     | Type   | Necessity | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Default |
| ---------- | ------ | --------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------- |
| type       | string | Required  | Human-readable label that identifies this field type. Value must be token.                                                                                                                                                                                        |         |
| normalizer | string | Optional  | Type of transformation to perform on the field value. Value can be one of the following:lowercase - to transform text values in string fields to lowercase.none - to not perform any transformation.If you don't set this option explicitly, it defaults to none. | none    |The following index definition examples use collections in the
[sample data sets](available-sample-datasets). After you
[Load Sample Data Into Atlas](load-sample-data), you can use
these examples to index fields as the dateFacet type.To learn how to set up and run the following example code, see
Define the Index for the token Type.Index as token Type Only ExampleThe following index definition indexes string values in the
`title` field as MongoDB Search dateFacet type and converts the
field value to `lowercase`, which allows you to do the
following:* Perform case-insensitive sort, as specified by the
  `normalizer`, on the `title` field.

* Run exact match queries on the `title` field using the
  following operators:

  - [equals](equals-ref)

  - [in](in-ref)

  - [range](range-ref)```python
from pymongo import MongoClient
from pymongo.operations import SearchIndexModel

# connect to your Atlas deployment
uri = "<connection-string>"
client = MongoClient(uri)

# set namespace
database = client["sample_mflix"]
collection = database["movies"]

# define your MongoDB Search index
search_index_model = SearchIndexModel(
    definition={
        "mappings": {
            "dynamic": false,
            "fields": {
                "title": {
                    "type": "token",
                    "normalizer": "lowercase"
                }
            }
        },
    },
    name="default",
)

# create the index
result = collection.create_search_index(model=search_index_model)
print(f"New index name: {result}")
```Index as Multiple Types ExampleThe following index definition indexes the `genres` field as
`string` and `token` types to return the following:* Search results for queries using MongoDB Search operators like
  [text](text-ref), [Phrase](phrase-ref), and other operators that
  perform text search on the `genres` field.

* Sorted results for queries using the [$search](pipe.$search)
  [sorting](sort-ref) option on the `genres` field.

* Exact matches for queries using MongoDB Search operators like
  [equals](equals-ref), [in](in-ref), and [range](range-ref).```python
from pymongo import MongoClient
from pymongo.operations import SearchIndexModel

# connect to your Atlas deployment
uri = "<connection-string>"
client = MongoClient(uri)

# set namespace
database = client["sample_mflix"]
collection = database["movies"]

# define your MongoDB Search index
search_index_model = SearchIndexModel(
    definition={
        "mappings": {
            "dynamic": False,
            "fields": {
                "genres": [
                    {
                        "type": "string"
                    },
                    {
                        "type": "token"
                    }
                ]
            }
        },
    },
    name="default",
)

# create the index
result = collection.create_search_index(model=search_index_model)
print(f"New index name: {result}")
```
